quiz_items.question: How is a laser product's Class determined?
options: By the laser medium used (He-Ne, CO2, etc.)
By the intended application (medical, industrial, etc.)
By the highest level of laser radiation to which human access is possible during operation
quiz_items.explanation: Laser products are classified based on the highest level of laser radiation to which human access is possible during operation only. Maintenance/service access levels don't affect classification but may affect labeling and interlock requirements.

quiz_items.question: A laser product has radiation accessible only during infrequent maintenance procedures. How does this affect classification?
options: The product must be classified based on maintenance access levels
The maintenance access level does not affect classification
The product is automatically Class IV
quiz_items.explanation: Human access during maintenance does not affect the product classification, though it may affect labeling and safety interlock requirements. Classification is based on highest accessible radiation during operation only.
